primary_focus: createPortal for rendering outside DOM hierarchy, ref-as-prop (React 19, no forwardRef), reusable component API design

portal_signals.trigger: Requirements 9, 14: render outside the table's DOM hierarchy
portal_signals.correct: Tooltip and popover content is rendered via createPortal to document.body or a dedicated portal container. This avoids clipping by overflow:hidden or z-index stacking context issues in the table.
portal_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: Rendering the tooltip/popover inline as a sibling of the trigger element inside the table. This is clipped by the table's overflow and stacking context.
portal_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: Using CSS position:fixed to escape the stacking context. This works visually but the tooltip is still in the table's DOM — accessibility tools may not present it correctly, and event bubbling passes through the table.

ref_signals.trigger: Requirement 18: reusable tooltip component that wraps a trigger element
ref_signals.correct: The tooltip/popover component accepts a ref prop to measure the trigger element's position. In React 19, ref is a regular prop — the component just declares ref in its props interface. No forwardRef wrapper needed.
ref_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: No ref usage — tooltip positioned with CSS relative to a wrapper div, which breaks when the trigger element varies in size or the page scrolls.
ref_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: Using React.forwardRef to pass the ref. This works but is the pre-React 19 pattern. In React 19, forwardRef is unnecessary and adds complexity.

ref_signals.trigger: Requirements 8, 13: position tooltips/popovers relative to trigger, flip if overflowing viewport
ref_signals.correct: Use ref.current.getBoundingClientRect() to get the trigger's position, then calculate tooltip position. Check against window.innerHeight/innerWidth to determine if flipping is needed.
ref_signals.anti_pattern: Hardcoded positioning or CSS-only positioning that doesn't handle viewport overflow

component_design_signals.trigger: Requirement 18: must be a reusable component, not hardcoded
component_design_signals.correct: A generic Tooltip component with props like: children (trigger element), content (tooltip content), placement ('top' | 'bottom'), delay, etc. Similarly a generic Popover component. Both handle portal rendering, positioning, and lifecycle internally.
component_design_signals.anti_pattern: Tooltip logic inlined in the table cell components. Works for this table but not reusable.

component_design_signals.signal: Separation of trigger and overlay logic
component_design_signals.correct: The tooltip/popover component manages its own show/hide state, delay timers, and positioning. The consuming component just wraps its trigger element.
component_design_signals.anti_pattern: The table component manages tooltip visibility state and passes it down

effect_hygiene_signals.correct: setTimeout for the delay, cleared in a cleanup function (onMouseLeave or useEffect cleanup). No lingering timers.
effect_hygiene_signals.anti_pattern: Timer not cleared on mouse leave — tooltip appears after the user has already moved away

effect_hygiene_signals.trigger: Requirement 15: Escape and click-outside close popover
effect_hygiene_signals.correct: useEffect adding document-level keydown and click listeners with proper cleanup on unmount or when popover closes
effect_hygiene_signals.anti_pattern: Global listeners added but never removed

accessibility_signals.correct: Trigger element has aria-describedby pointing to the tooltip's id. Tooltip has role='tooltip'.
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: No ARIA linking — tooltip is visual-only

accessibility_signals.correct: Popover menu items are focusable. Arrow keys move between items. Enter selects. Escape closes and returns focus to the trigger button.
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: Menu items only clickable, not keyboard-navigable

accessibility_signals.correct: <table> with <thead>/<tbody>, <th scope='col'> for headers, sortable headers have aria-sort

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: sortedUsers
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Computed via useMemo from USERS, sortColumn, and sortDirection
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.anti_pattern: Stored as useState and synced via useEffect

legitimate_effect_uses.purpose: Document-level event listeners for click-outside and Escape
Recalculate tooltip/popover position on scroll or resize
legitimate_effect_uses.expected_pattern: useEffect with scroll/resize listeners and cleanup
